primary_accounting_issue: Whether foreign exchange risk on intercompany dividend receivables can qualify for hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements

primary_authority.reason: Paragraph 6.3.5 restricts hedge accounting to items with external parties, while paragraph 6.3.6 provides an exception for intragroup monetary items that create FX exposure not eliminated on consolidation

Establishes that intragroup monetary items cannot be eliminated on consolidation without recognizing currency fluctuation effects, creating the FX exposure that paragraph 6.3.6 of IFRS 9 permits to be hedged

supporting_authority.reason: Clarifies that foreign currency hedging instruments may be designated for FX risk in net investment hedges and defines the eligible hedged risk as FX differences between functional currencies

peripheral_authority.reason: Addresses disclosure requirements for interests in subsidiaries and restrictions but does not govern hedge accounting eligibility for intragroup items
Addresses measurement of dividend payables but not hedge accounting treatment of dividend receivables
Addresses disclosure of liabilities arising from financing activities but not hedge accounting eligibility

treatment_families.family: Hedge accounting for intragroup monetary items
General foreign exchange accounting without hedge accounting

approaches.label: Hedge accounting for intragroup monetary items
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.6 permits hedge accounting for intragroup monetary items (including dividend receivables) when the FX exposure is not fully eliminated on consolidation, subject to external party requirement and formal documentation under IFRS 9 paragraph 6.4.1

approaches.label: Foreign exchange accounting without hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: When hedge accounting is not elected or not available, IAS 21 paragraph 45 requires recognition of FX gains/losses on intragroup monetary items in profit or loss (or OCI if related to disposal of foreign operation) without hedge accounting treatment
